{{Infobox character
| name = Mad King
| appears_in = Cradle
| titles = Mad King
| abilities = Makiel, savage technique
| introduced = cradle-07-uncrowned
}}

''See also: [[Mad King Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[The Mad King]], formerly known as the hero [[Daruman]], is a powerful and notorious figure characterized by his role as a conqueror and imperialist. Once an [[Executor]] recruited by the [[Abidan]], he was later branded with his current title after his will was corrupted by [[Oth’kimeth]]. He is a significant enemy of the [[Abidan]], driven by a desire to destroy them and dismantle their influence. His combat prowess is immense; he wields a dark Scythe and a bone [[sword]] that carries the sorrow of many worlds, and he has demonstrated the ability to slice an [[Iteration]] in half. Despite his destructive nature, he is known to be a generous leader to his own people and maintains a citadel at the fortress-world of [[Tal’gullour]]. Physically, he has been depicted in black armor carved from the physical remains of [[Oth’kimeth]], featuring a bone helmet and red eyes. He possesses immense power, including the ability to manifest chaotic fire and manipulate reality, though his existence has become unstable due to repeated deaths and the need for self-revival. He is also associated with a [[Fiend]] sealed within him and has utilized a cursed virus to spread chaos. While he is a formidable threat capable of challenging high-level entities like [[Makiel]] and [[Ozriel]], his physical state has occasionally been compromised, leaving him with scarred madra channels and a weakened lifeline.}}

== Uncrowned ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Vroshir]] known as [[the Mad King]] possesses an [[aura]] that resembles a blazing, implacable wave of magma-like chaos. He is clad in armor meticulously carved from the physical manifestation of a Class One [[Fiend]]. During his assault, he struck three additional times after removing the first world. He further led a charge while wielding a false Scythe.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[The Mad King]] is a [[Vroshir]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c18">''Uncrowned'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[The Mad King]]'s [[aura]] is a blazing, implacable wave of chaos like magma.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[The Mad King]] wears armor carved from the physical manifestation of a Class One [[Fiend]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[The Mad King]] struck three more times after removing the first world.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c21">''Uncrowned'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[The Mad King]] leads a charge with a false Scythe.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c21" />}}

== Bloodline ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Daruman]] was given the title of [[the Mad King]] by the [[Court]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c6">''Bloodline'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[The Mad King]] is an exception among the [[Vroshir]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18">''Bloodline'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[The Mad King]]'s workings can cast [[Makiel]] through space.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19">''Bloodline'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[The Mad King]] exploited an opening to cast a wave of chaotic many-colored fire across the planet.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19" />}}

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[The Mad King]], revealed to be [[Daruman]], manifests into reality wielding a black [[scythe]]. A figure whose arrival signals that something has gone terribly wrong, he commands through his spoken word and remains unshaken even while containing the [[Fiend]] [[Oth’kimeth]]. Seeking to strike a blow against and topple the pillars of Heaven, he battles the [[Judges]] of the [[Abidan Court]]. Despite being an old enemy of [[Ozriel]], he fled during the strike on the shroud, which he himself had created.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[The Mad King]] manifests into reality holding a black Scythe.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c3">''Reaper'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[The Mad King]] contains the [[Fiend]] [[Oth’kimeth]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c15">''Reaper'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[The Mad King]] is a character who commands with his word.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[The Mad King]] did not tremble.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[The Mad King]] sought to strike a blow and topple the pillars of Heaven.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[The Mad King]] battles the [[Judges]] of the [[Abidan Court]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c22">''Reaper'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[The Mad King]] is an old enemy of [[Ozriel]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c25">''Reaper'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[The Mad King]] fled during the strike on the shroud.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[The Mad King]] made the [[Origin Shroud]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c26">''Reaper'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[The Mad King]] is [[Daruman]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c26" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[The Mad King]] uses a [[scythe]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c26" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[The Mad King]] is a figure whose arrival signifies something has gone terribly wrong.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c27">''Reaper'', chapter 27</ref>}}

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|A figure capable of crafting lethal weaponry, [[the Mad King]] famously produced a copy of [[Ozriel]]'s own weapon. His mere presence possesses the power to warp [[Fate]], and he is a person whom [[Ozriel]] specifically intended to strain through their relationship. As he prepares to ride to war once more, there are indications that he may have left a [[Vroshir]] behind to facilitate movement without detection.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[The Mad King]] copied [[Ozriel]]'s weapon.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c1">''Dreadgod'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[The Mad King]] is capable of creating deadly weapons.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[The Mad King]] is a figure whose relationship [[Ozriel]] intended to strain.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c6">''Dreadgod'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[The Mad King]] warps [[Fate]] by his very presence.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[The Mad King]] intends to ride to war once more.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c20">''Dreadgod'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[The Mad King]] may have left a [[Vroshir]] behind to move undetected.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c24">''Dreadgod'', chapter 24</ref>}}

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[The Mad King]] is an intruder clad in bone armor who becomes a central figure of suspicion for the [[Abidan]] regarding the Reapers. While [[Suriel]] intends to meet him, he is seen engaged in combat against two [[Judges]], an encounter that necessitates [[Makiel]]'s preparation. Although a fragment of him floats within the Void, his true self serves as a combatant in a struggle against [[Ozriel]]. During this battle, [[the Mad King]] utilizes a savage technique to defend against [[Eithan]]'s strikes and suffers half of his body being erased by the [[Scythe of Ozriel]]. His history is further marked by promises made to [[Li Markuth]], who responded by placing a curse upon him.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[The Mad King]] is a character [[Suriel]] intends to meet.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c1">''Waybound'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[The Mad King]] was engaged in a fight with two [[Judges]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6">''Waybound'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Makiel]] must be prepared to face [[the Mad King]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|A fragment of [[the Mad King]] is floating in the Void.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c18">''Waybound'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The true self of [[the Mad King]] is fighting [[Ozriel]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[The Mad King]] is a combatant fighting [[Ozriel]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c22">''Waybound'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[The Mad King]] uses a savage technique to defend against [[Eithan]]'s strikes.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c27">''Waybound'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[The Mad King]] is a character whose body was half-erased by the [[Scythe of Ozriel]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c29">''Waybound'', chapter 29</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[The Mad King]] is a figure used as a reason for the [[Abidan]]'s suspicion of the Reapers.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c32">''Waybound'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[The Mad King]] made promises to [[Li Markuth]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c35">''Waybound'', chapter 35</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Li Markuth]] cursed [[the Mad King]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c35" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[The Mad King]] is an intruder in bone armor.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c36">''Waybound'', chapter 36</ref>}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[The Mad King]]'s actions caused damage to worlds.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c7">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Vall]] lost an eye in a battle with [[the Mad King]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[The Mad King]] was driven away.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c14">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 14</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|“Come,” the Mad King commanded, and he was echoed by his Fiend.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c15" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|“I sought to strike you a blow. Never did I imagine that I could topple the pillars of Heaven,”<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c15" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|“Daruman, the Mad King!” Eithan shouted.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c26" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|“They have reason to think so, considering the Mad King.”<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c32" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle characters]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
